observation.summary: Architecture docs, migration docs, and roadmap changes are observed through path-scoped CI.

hypothesis.summary: A doc change can introduce stale boundary wording or break executable SSOT links.

execution.summary: Run the architecture verifier and all architecture tool tests.

evaluation.summary: The verifier fails on missing required files, stale wording, dependency drift, or failed tool tests.

retrospective.summary: Closed decisions move into owning SSOT docs; unresolved shared decisions stay in the roadmap.

evidence.proves: architecture docs remain executable and linked to tool checks

observation.summary: Unresolved execution risks are represented as local, external, or remaining-boundary evidence.

hypothesis.summary: Every claimed risk closure must point at a local test, external repo evidence, or an explicit remaining boundary.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects missing risks, private boundary leaks, missing tests, and unmentioned evidence.

retrospective.summary: New unresolved findings become manifest rows before implementation claims closure.

evidence.proves: agent execution risk claims are machine checked

observation.summary: Store channel claims are observed through a package/distribution contract.

hypothesis.summary: A distribution change can accidentally add forbidden artifacts, terms, or user-path leakage.

execution.summary: Run the store contract verifier against the repository.

evaluation.summary: The verifier fails on missing docs, notice terms, forbidden package roots, or user-path artifacts.

retrospective.summary: Store review and channel decisions are promoted into C11 SSOT instead of loose notes.

observation.summary: Real provider CLI availability and integration status are observed through a scheduled/manual evidence artifact.

hypothesis.summary: Public runners may have no provider CLIs, but that must be recorded as observed skip rather than invisible absence.

execution.summary: Run the provider observation tool with integration enabled and upload the evidence JSON artifact.

evaluation.summary: Missing executables produce skipped evidence; detected providers must pass TestIntegration or fail the workflow.

retrospective.summary: Provider matrix wording points to generated observation evidence instead of a future unscheduled idea.

evidence.proves: provider observation docs are generated from the manifest
public CI records scheduled/manual real CLI observation artifacts

observation.summary: Secret redaction decisions are observed through C7 policy, ToolRef args, and C2 EventIngestor tests.

hypothesis.summary: Security docs must not redefine redaction markers outside the redaction SSOT.

execution.summary: Run redaction/audit tests, toolargs/toolpolicy tests, and the doc drift verifier.

evaluation.summary: Tests prove marker generation, safe-value behavior, second-pass redaction, PolicyViolationDetected audit append, and no marker/catalog redefinition outside the SSOT.

retrospective.summary: When security hub docs need redaction details, they link to the redaction SSOT instead of restating marker/catalog rules.

evidence.proves: security docs do not redefine redaction marker/catalog details outside the SSOT

open_gaps.current_handling: public daemon docs describe metadata-only secret evidence, but the collector/tool is outside this repository
open_gaps.required_next_artifact: private infra evidence packet or public stub manifest that proves no raw secret collection path
